Details.value: Reception 3:00–5:00 PM. Call (607) 210-2655 in advance if arriving outside those hours or for contactless check-in (Mon–Fri, 9:00 AM–4:30 PM EST).
Firewood ($10/bundle) and s'mores kit at the cottage.
Build your plan in the Itinerary tab — one main activity, lunch, car nap, one afternoon stop.
Lakewood Vineyards is the safest family-friendly default.

Notes.value: Good first-night waterfront dinner; keep it simple.
Go early; carrier better than stroller on gorge stairs.
Large lawn; easy kid snack stop while one adult tastes.
Easy kid reset after winery.
Best rainy/too-hot option for ages 2 and 5.
Big playground; pair with Purity Ice Cream.
Optional PM stop if timing works; head home to Saratoga Springs after.

Name.value: Drive The Glen · Watkins Glen International

Notes.value: 3 escorted laps in your car. Book ahead; July 6–10 clear.
Racing history, photos, model cars, short film. Pair with historic street circuit.
Follow marked signs on public roads — the original 1948 street course through downtown.
Must-do. Many stairs.
Playground, lake, easy parking.
Large playground.
Easy overlook, minimal walking.
Quick scenic stop.
Good low-effort reset.
Pick a short trail only.
Easy short stop.
Main grocery run after Monday check-in. Mon list: Zweigle's Red & Whites (Tue lunch), buns, mustard, snacks.
Milk, snacks, basics only — not a full grocery run.
Waterfront; good first night.
Easy kid food.
Reliable kid option.
Post-park treat.
Closest easy food.
Local casual stop.
Pair with Sciencenter.
Good adult/kid compromise.
Easy after glass museum.
Closest real breakfast diner. Homemade pancakes & gravy. Closed Mondays.
Smoked meats, baked goods, local farm ingredients. Fri–Mon 9 AM–3 PM.
Local staple since 1955. Open daily 8 AM–3 PM. Great before gorge day.
Handmade donuts since 1952 + full breakfast. 2 blocks from state park.
Downtown bagels & breakfast sandwiches. Good pre-gorge coffee stop.
Rustic lodge by the gorge. Racing memorabilia bar; family-friendly dining.
Old-school diner. Breakfast all day; salad bar at lunch.
Down-home spot. Breakfast served all day; kids menu.
T-burg living room since 1988. Early breakfast Tue–Sun.
Creative local café. Fri–Sat 9–2, Sun 9–1 — pair with Ithaca day.
Franklin St brewpub. Flatbreads, tacos, kid-friendly bar food.
Racing-themed seafood spot. Better for lunch with the 5yo car fan.
LEGO, games, puzzles — best kid shop on the trip.
Same great toy store on Market St near glass museum.
Bulk candy and chocolate on North Franklin St.
Fudge and truffles made on site.
Funny gifts and local crafts on Franklin St.
Toys and gorge souvenirs at park entrance.
Racing books and merch at free IMRRC museum.
Walkable Market St shops after glass museum.
Huge shop — glass ornaments and kid-friendly pieces.
Pedestrian commons — browse on way to Alphabet Soup.

Why.value: Best rainy day for both kids.
Free indoor racing history — car-kid rainy backup (Mon–Fri).
